reviews.author: Paul Parent
reviews.text: ChatGPT said: Doi Nhai Beach, located in the northern part of Vung Tau, is a quieter and more rustic alternative to the city’s busier stretches of coastline. Far from the tourist crowds, it offers a more authentic and laid-back atmosphere, making it a favorite among locals who come here to relax, fish, or enjoy a simple picnic by the sea. The beach is wide and sandy, bordered by shallow waters that stretch far out during low tide, revealing tidal flats dotted with small fishing boats. This creates a peaceful, open landscape where the horizon seems endless. In the early morning, the scene is particularly charming, with fishermen casting their nets and the soft light reflecting off the gentle waves. Unlike the more developed Front and Back Beaches, Doi Nhai retains a raw, natural feel. There are fewer amenities, which adds to its appeal for those seeking a slower pace and a genuine connection with the local way of life. Small seafood stalls and family-run eateries nearby serve freshly caught fish and shellfish, often grilled to order and best enjoyed with a sea breeze. Evenings at Doi Nhai are tranquil, with the sounds of the ocean and the sight of distant lights shimmering across the water. It’s not a beach for luxury or high-energy entertainment, but rather for moments of quiet beauty, cultural authenticity, and a reminder of Vung Tau’s fishing heritage.

reviews.author: Michele Lauri
reviews.text: The road to the beach is really raidy and dirty. The bay is really beautiful, but unfortunately the beach is often full of waste coming from the sea. However, the area is quiet and perfect for Kite Surfing

reviews.author: kitesurfing lessons vietnam
reviews.text: The beach can look quite different if during low or high tide. I must admit that the sea brings plastics, bottles and different stuff and unfortunately the municipality don't care about it. Some people spend the night mostly at weekends under a camping tent. Also some kitesurfers visit the spot.

reviews.author: Travel dad
reviews.text: If you want to catch sea creatures and play on a very large beach low tide then this is your place. Time it so you’re there an hour before the tide is the lowest. Then have fun. It’s great for kids of all age.

reviews.author: paul rowe
reviews.text: An excellent open beach. Safe swimming. Good for kites.

reviews.author: BTB - Break the box
reviews.text: It is pretty good if your’re using the fish catching to experience, the ocean water is a bit dirty, but the worst thing here is if you sleep here at night. Mosquitoes will come out and bite you till they die

reviews.author: Mr M xxx
reviews.text: A relaxing day. Not much to do but relax. It's a nice little spot.

reviews.author: James Huynh
reviews.text: Local beach. Nice and friendly. Need to be cleaned more often. Camping friendly

reviews.author: drummerAsso 87
reviews.text: What a pity... a great delusion. The beach is really dirty. They don’t really care to keep cleaning this beautiful sand.

reviews.author: Trang Nguyen
reviews.text: Doi Nhai beach has a nice simple view for visitors to explore

reviews.author: Karl Gledhill
reviews.text: What a pity! Some of the pictures put up previously makes this place look amazing! In contrast it was full of litter on the beach. The local area is not in good condition and litter can be seen all around. Really disappointing

reviews.author: Hoa Nguyen
reviews.text: Wild place but very dirty, less support facility

reviews.author: Quý Nguyễn
reviews.text: Fun for off-road activities over the beautiful sunny weekend

reviews.author: Canhchimhaiau59
reviews.text: The best place for fishing at sea side. I was delighted to spend my time here with friends.

reviews.author: Tu Dang
reviews.text: This part of beach is so full of trash... Explains why not a lot of people go here...
